'''Jeremy Smith''' (born 14 April 1980) is a New Zealand former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. A [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] international representative, he played as a {{rlp|sr}} and {{rlp|lk}}. He played for the [[Melbourne Storm]], the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], with whom he won the [[2010 NRL Grand Final]] with, the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] and the [[Newcastle Knights]], who he co-captained, in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].

==Background==
Of [[Samoans|Samoan]] heritage,<ref>[http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,23059606-23214,00.html]   {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080705191512/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0%2C8659%2C23059606-23214%2C00.html|date=5 July 2008}}</ref> Smith was born on 14 April 1980, in [[Christchurch]]. He is a cousin of [[Kalifa Faifai Loa]].<ref name="NZ_Herald_10722231">{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10722231&ref=rss|title=NRL: Smith cleared of Hopoate-like poke|date=29 April 2011|agency=[[Australian Associated Press]]|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=6 October 2011}}</ref> Smith was educated at [[Coombabah State High School]].

Smith played his junior football for the [[Altona Roosters]] in [[Melbourne, Victoria]]<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.starweekly.com.au/uncategorized/279059-vrl-altona-roosters-plan-to-make-it-a-year-to-remember/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160121233250/http://www.starweekly.com.au/uncategorized/279059-vrl-altona-roosters-plan-to-make-it-a-year-to-remember/|url-status=dead|archive-date=21 January 2016|title=Altona Roosters plan to make it a year to remember|author=Jenkinson, Lance|newspaper=Maribyrnong Star Weekly|date=19 April 2015 }}</ref> before moving to [[Queensland]] to play for [[Runaway Bay Seagulls]] RLFC.

In 2001, Smith was signed by the [[Northern Eagles]], but was sacked by coach [[Peter Sharp (rugby league)|Peter Sharp]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Smith sacked by Manly and his Melbourne NRL lifeline |url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2019/07/26/smith-sacked-by-manly-and-his-melbourne-nrl-lifeline/ |website=newcastleknights.com.au |publisher=[[Newcastle Knights]] |access-date=14 March 2022 |date=26 July 2019}}</ref> He returned to [[Queensland]], eventually finding his way to play for [[Melbourne Storm]] feeder club, [[Norths Devils]] in the [[Queensland Cup]]. In 2004, Smith was named Devils Player of the Year.

==Professional playing career==
===Melbourne Storm===
In round 23 of the [[2004 Melbourne Storm season|2004 NRL season]], Smith made his [[NRL]] debut for the [[Melbourne Storm]] against the [[Canberra Raiders]]. He scored a try on debut. Smith didn't play first-grade in 2005 but became a regular first-grader in 2006. The Storm reached the [[2006 NRL Grand Final]] and Smith was selected to play from the interchange bench in the loss to the [[Brisbane Broncos|Broncos]]. He played in the [[2007 NRL Grand Final]] win over the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Sea Eagles]], again selected off the interchange. In 2010, the premiership that Smith won with Melbourne was stripped by the [[NRL]] due to the club's major and deliberate breaches of the salary cap.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/storm/they-are-ours-why-cameron-smith-will-always-recognise-storms-stripped-premierships/news-story/6397becb6e5091d6804ef91c578c56bd/|title='They are ours': Why Cameron Smith will always recognise Storm's stripped premierships|website=Fox Sports|date=31 July 2019}}</ref>
 
After winning the [[2007 NRL Grand Final]] with the [[Melbourne Storm]], Smith was selected for the [[2007 All Golds tour]]. Not to be confused with compatriot and namesake [[Jeremy Smith (rugby league born 1981)|Jeremy James Smith]], both Jeremy Smiths were named in the [[rugby league]] Centenary clash of 14 October 2007, Jeremy James Smith in the number 7 jersey and Jeremy Jon Oscar Smith in the number 13 jersey.<ref>{{cite news| url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7032300.stm| work=[[BBC News]] | title=Kiwis unveil new-look Test side| date=2007-10-07}}</ref><ref name="League: Smith has big shoes to fill">{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/section/4/story.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10468776|title=League: Smith has big shoes to fill|publisher=[[The New Zealand Herald]] |date=2007-10-09|access-date=2008-08-02|first=Robert|last=Lowe}}</ref>
[[File:Jeremy Smith.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Smith playing for the New Zealand at the 2008 RLWC]]
In August 2008, Smith was named in the New Zealand training squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Kiwis select Sonny Bill">{{cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949034,00.html|title=Kiwis select Sonny Bill|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-08-08|access-date=2008-08-08}}</ref> Later in the season Smith played in the [[2008 NRL Grand Final]] defeat by Manly, starting in the {{rlp|sr}}.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2008/grand-final/manly-warringah-sea-eagles-vs-melbourne-storm/summary.html |title=2008 NRL Telstra Premiership: Grand Final |website=Rugby League Project |access-date=11 March 2026}}</ref> Smith signed a 3-year contract with the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] starting in 2009 to be a part of the new [[Wayne Bennett (rugby league)|Wayne Bennett]] era at the Dragons.<ref name="2009 NRL Player Movements">{{cite web|url=http://www.nrllive.com.au/2009-nrl-player-movements/|title=2009 NRL Player Movements|publisher=NRL Live|date=2008-10-04|access-date=2008-10-04|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081005172545/http://www.nrllive.com.au/2009-nrl-player-movements/|archive-date=5 October 2008|df=dmy }}</ref> In October 2008, he was named in the final 24-man [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#New Zealand|New Zealand Kiwis]] squad.<ref name="Kiwis to wait on Webb and Matai">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7656238.stm|title=Kiwis to wait on Webb and Matai|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-07|access-date=2008-10-07}}</ref> Smith was a member of the World Cup-winning New Zealand team beating [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in the [[2008 World Cup final]] at [[Suncorp Stadium]] in [[Brisbane, Queensland]]. In 2010, the premiership that Smith won with Melbourne in 2007 was stripped by the NRL for major and deliberate breaches of the salary cap.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/melbourne-storm-stripped-of-premierships-for-salary-cap-breaches-20100422-td91.html|title = Melbourne Storm stripped of premierships for salary cap breaches|date = 22 April 2010}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2010-04-23/storm-punishment-too-harsh-geyer/407390|title = Storm punishment too harsh: Geyer|newspaper = ABC News|date = 22 April 2010}}</ref>

===St. George Illawarra Dragons===
In January 2009, Smith was named the 2008 [[New Zealand Rugby League]] Player of the Year.<ref name="Jeremy Smith named Kiwi player of the year">{{cite web|url=http://www.livenews.com.au/Articles/2009/02/01/Jeremy_Smith_named_Kiwi_player_of_the_year|title=Jeremy Smith named Kiwi player of the year|publisher=Macquarie National News|date=2009-02-02|access-date=2009-02-02|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090205120152/http://livenews.com.au/Articles/2009/02/01/Jeremy_Smith_named_Kiwi_player_of_the_year|archive-date=5 February 2009|df=dmy-all}}</ref> In his first year at the Dragons, the team won the 2009 Minor Premiership after finishing first in the regular season and again won the Minor Premiership in 2010. In August 2010, Smith signed a 3-year contract with the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] starting in 2011.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/cronulla-sharks-snare-jeremy-smith-from-arch-rival-st-george-illawarra-dragons/story-e6frexnr-1225903813520|title=Cronulla Sharks Snare Jeremy Smith From Arch Rivals St. George Illawarra|publisher=Dailytelgraph.com.au|access-date=2015-06-06}}</ref> Smith played at {{rlp|lk}} in the Dragons' team that won the [[2010 NRL Grand Final]], defeating the [[Sydney Roosters]]. This was Smith's first valid premiership. After that win, he had played in more grand finals than any other New Zealander.<ref name="NZ_Herald_10716794">{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10716794&ref=rss|title=NRL: Second to no one with attitude|author=Burgess, Michael|date=3 April 2011|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=6 October 2011}}</ref> After winning the [[2010 NRL Grand Final]] with the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], Smith was a part of New Zealand's successful [[2010 Four Nations]] campaign.

[[File:Jeremy Smith Storm.jpg|thumb|right|Smith playing for the [[Melbourne Storm]] in 2008]]

===Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks===
Despite having a disappointing year with the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] in 2011, Smith was again chosen to represent New Zealand in the [[2011 ANZAC Test]] and [[2011 Four Nations]]. After a successful start to year with the Sharks, Smith was selected in the [[2012 ANZAC Test]]. On 26 September 2012, Smith signed a three-year contract with the [[Newcastle Knights]] starting in 2013 to rejoin his former Dragons coach, [[Wayne Bennett (rugby league)|Wayne Bennett]].<ref>[http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Knights-sign-Jeremy-Smith/64513] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120930090330/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Knights-sign-Jeremy-Smith/64513|date=30 September 2012}}</ref> He was released from the final year of his Sharks contract for personal reasons.<ref name="Sharks grant Smith release">{{cite web|url=http://www.sharks.com.au/news-display/Sharks-grant-Smith-release/64448|title=Sharks grant Smith release|publisher=sharks.com.au|date=31 December 2011|access-date=26 September 2012|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121006063554/http://www.sharks.com.au/news-display/Sharks-grant-Smith-release/64448|archive-date=6 October 2012}}</ref> He was named the [[New Zealand Rugby League]]'s 2012 Player of the Year.<ref>[http://www.stuff.co.nz/the-press/sport/rugby-league/7920852/Jeremy-Smith-named-Kiwis-player-of-the-year Jeremy Smith named Kiwis' player of the year] ''stuff.co.nz'', 8 November 2012</ref>

===Newcastle Knights===
Smith commenced playing with the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[2013 NRL season]].

On 22 August 2014, Smith became one of the current NRL players and former Sharks players to accept reduced bans from the [[Australian Sports Anti-Doping Authority]] for his role in the club's 2011 [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks supplements saga|supplements program]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/cronulla-sharks-players-accept-doping-bans-20140822-10728y.html|title=Cronulla Sharks players accept doping bans|publisher=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]] |date=22 August 2014|access-date=29 August 2014}}</ref>

On 13 February 2015, Smith played for the [[NRL All Stars team|NRL All Stars]] against the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] in the annual [[2015 All Stars match|All Stars match]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/final-all-stars-teams-announced/tabid/10874/newsid/83748/default.aspx|title=Final All Stars teams announced|publisher=NRL.com|date=2015-02-09|access-date=2015-06-06}}</ref> On 6 June 2015, he re-signed with the Knights on a 1-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2015/06/06/smith_to_end_career_.html|title=Smith signs on for one more - Knights|date=6 June 2015|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2015-06-06}}</ref>

On 26 February 2016, Smith was announced as one of three co-captains for the Knights alongside [[Trent Hodkinson]] and [[Tariq Sims]].<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/content/knights/club/news/2016/02/26/newcastle_knights_an.html|title=Newcastle Knights announce 2016 captains - Knights|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2016-02-26|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304131828/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/content/knights/club/news/2016/02/26/newcastle_knights_an.html|archive-date=4 March 2016|df=dmy-all}}</ref> On 13 May 2016, he announced his retirement from rugby league at the end of the 2016 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/smith-to-retire-at-end-of-season-21860/#bcIHtZHmQdKEFv0J.97|title=Smith to retire at end of season|publisher=Zero Tackle|access-date=2016-05-13|archive-date=18 August 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818135819/http://www.zerotackle.com/smith-to-retire-at-end-of-season-21860/#bcIHtZHmQdKEFv0J.97|url-status=dead}}</ref>
